channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi… — 139 replies — 556.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi…

Open thread • Replies: 139 • Updated: 2026-06-25 08:39 UTC

OP

>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago
>been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasing him
>haven't found any jobs, its unreal how bad the market is, have this bad feeling its never going to get better
>PIP ends on Tuesday
I am an engineer. I started off as a tech and worked my ass off getting bachelors full time to escape manual labor. Now im gonna be that goy with a masters degree who took a 500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work. I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.

Early replies (first 3)

62388691
>>62388676
>500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work
Trades pay more than ever, especially vs pajeet-saturated office work. Skill issue

62388694
>>62388676
My entire life has been nothing but fighting tooth and nail, working myself 1000x harder than anyone anywhere ever for 0.001% of what anyone else gets for free. I have felt that god is purposely shitting on me pretty much my whole life. It doesn't get better, it only ever gets worse. If anything happens to me that seem to be a good thing, I have 6 months max to enjoy it because that'll be taken from me and then I'll be beaten up and crushed down for added effect. It's a real comedy out there.

So don't worry bro, you have a lot of time ahead of you to suffer far more. If there really is a god, he must be the most perfectly evil of entities.

62388697
The worst part is that in my meeting with my boss today he didnt mention if I will pass the PIP. He said he needed to talk with some people including HR. So 90 percent chance he is going to fire me but just enough chance he wont that I have to spend the weekend waiting instead of processing the pain. And if he doesnt fire me, I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks. Like a absurd housewife that has no were else to go and she tries to esapce and gets rapped(applies to ghost jobs) even worse so she comes back to him. Thats me. I used to think my relationship with work was like between a man and a woman, were I am the man that picks and chooses women and is the one who leaves but its all turned around past couple of years. I am nothing but a side pieace female whore to these companies now, they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one. They got us with not giving raises to match inflation, they got us with return to work mandates and now, they are going to get us by firing us and leaving us with nothing. U will get put on a PIP too, at the worst time possible also. They wont even lay u off anymore, they want to save a little bit of money by denying us unemployment insurance by firing us through a PIP.


Was being in ur 30s a good excperince back in the day? There is no way human civilization would have gotten to were it is if it was always this bad for everyone. Of course, I am a faggot and just suck but the fsct of the matter is that PIPs and no jobs has been a very common trope on the internet lately

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62395938
>>62395717
>>62395641

It's actually more about your boss. 90% of bosses don't gaf as long as you generally do what they say. There is a 10% of proactive dicks who are absolutely looking to roll heads.

62398374
>>62395927
Just give them wfh and treat them like human beings, engineers dont mind working overtime if its for the right person.


Also ye i had similar dreams to start a plesant working place even at the expense of profits but I mostly want to do it out of spite at this point

62399956
>>62388711
>ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
jewish daycare

62399988
>>62388694
I hold LINK too, brother

62400021
>>62388676
I was in a similar position like you OP. I disagreed with the staff engineer, he bad mouthed me to manager and I landed on a PIP. I spent 6 months on a PIP, looking for a job (I'm in Germany, they can't really fire me).
Mental was the hardest part. This is a process designed to break you down. What really helped me was to disengage with the work process, say nothing, do the bare minimum, wear my poker face.
After 6 months, I found a new job, negotiated an exit with a severance package.
I am not gonna lie, this was a shitty experience, but I learned a lot from it.

I wish you best of luck OP. Don't let them wear you down.

62401552
>>62388676
AI training is a decent stopgap while you're looking for work. Mercor and Micro1 are best, Handshake's okay, Outlier sucks but doesn't pull shenanigans with your pay (I've heard that Alignerr does).

62402291
>>62400021
Dude im at like 8 months in total i just a took a month long break at the baggining of the year. I would have found a job by now.. I sidnt even disagree with anybody, they are just bullying me and breaking my spirit. Idk why they haven't fired me ye.t today is the last day of my PIP and I have heard nothing. I really just wanna ask my boss if they are gonna can me or not

62404970
>>62402291
Well, what happened?

62405224
>>62388694
>working myself 1000x harder
No, let's bring you back to reality. You're on /biz/ and the type of hard work you stated is reading white papers and then falling for the next make-it-big scam. You should have chased an honest life, but you're gambling and that does not pay.

62405704
>>62393491
Unemployment usually pays out. The only time it doesn’t is if you get fired for not showing up.

62406146
>>62404970
PIP ended yesterday and nothing so far. I won't be out of the clear until week after next, they could just be finalizing paperwork. If they dont fire me it will be hilarious beacuse they have been acting exactly like they would

62406163
>>62405704
That makes sence cuz the only time i was denied was when I was too sick to show up for work for 2 weeks

62407227
>>62399956
WebMs don't have sound on this board, so here's the original: https://x.com/LindyTasteful/status/2044400550083715317

62407295
>>62388992
Changing your outlook is about changing how you relate to the world, not making the world more comfortable with you. If he's happy (or at least happierk) raging, he should.

62407320
>>62390565
Organize a union.

62407396
Reading this thread has really changed my opinion of the average American. I didn’t know you were all suffering like this. I finally understand Trump voters, I thought they were simply NPCs susceptible to marketing. They’re just stressed to the limit and want to blow the system up no matter what. Makes a bit more sense

I’m going to be nicer to everyone I meet and be more grateful for what I have.

62407517
>>62407396
Nah a lot of it is still NPCs susceptible to marketing. And racism. That said, even as a hardcore progressive, I'll be the first to tell you that Biden made things immeasurably worse, and waterboard me with milk a few times and I'll even admit that Obama didn't do as much for the American people as he could or should have. I genuinely wish he'd been the kind of full-blown socialist globalism clean energy sellout nigger anger monkey he got portrayed as, because then at least we'd have renewable energy and free college to go along with printing and spending trillions of dollars we're never going to get back (we used it on wars and PPP loans asset inflation instead).

I understand the desire to seriously reconsider some fundamental institutions.

62407709
>>62388711
>all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries
>>62399956

boomers always have the last word on new hires, and they love young woman, I think you can guess why. Boomer niggers will be 85 years old in a wheelchair and still view 18 year olds as their sexual rivals

62407855
>>62388762
This actually the ”i want to attract chad” pose. T. A chad

62408366
>>62407709
This is related to the main reason underqualified men get hired when they do: it's when the boss sees them as a protege. This is why it's useless to apply places where affinity bias isn't working in your favor, and why it's way easier for tall men to get hired. Forget your CV, forget your technical interview; what matters is making the boss feel like they're looking at a younger version of themselves. They want to imbue with their knowledge, so that you can avoid the mistakes they made, and be a sort of redo of their early career.

62408409
>>62407517
Kys commie faggot

62408428
>62408409
A~nd filtered! :)

62408434
>>62388706
The pay is great, you have been reading fake news. And Id rather use my body than get gamer spine sitting around all day like some useless faggot pretending to work all day. Have you even adjusted your calculations for how little respect you earn as a desk faggot? You literally sit there boring your eyes out looking at a screen as your back disintegrates from no use, get fat, your wife leaves you for a tradie who still has vemm and verve while you are a beam counter husk for some globohomo pozzed company thats actively trying to design the tech that will replace you. Have fun.

62408443
>>62408366
>The 70 year old boomers "see themselves in" attractive, retarded young women
DESU the demographic collapse could be solved if young men just did this. Nothing sexier than giving out cushy sinecures.

62408445
>>62407517
Degenerates like you belong in a woodchipper

62408451
>>62408366
Underqualified men get hired because they work for indian tier wages, end of story.

62408561
>>62388676
>PIP ends on Tuesday
My unemployment ends in five weeks so I'm in the same boat as you OP. Except I'm a mongoloid with no degree living in the deep south so maybe I'll manage out in the woods.
>I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.
Probably not God causing all this, though a group of people that God has described in the past as rebellious and stiff necked may have something to do with this global cascade of misfortune that is harming both you and the rest of us. I have nothing to give you OP other than don't give up because shit's getting silly and the most interesting/funny worst days may be ahead of us. Try and lay low and live light so you can cruise through the potential coming calamity with peace of mind and a lot of lulz.

62408699
>>62408443
>This is related to the main reason underqualified men get hired when they do
Pls read anon
Though I suppose they do see themselves "in" those women, in way.

62408739
>>62390675
honestly it's not a bad wage for an double digit IQ person who couldn't afford college

62410030
>>62406146
they are dragging out the process to mentally wear you down
Don't let them

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 50 replies — 128.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-25 08:17 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62401192
I hold 3.4btc. is it enough?

62401205
>>62395655
Token not needed.

62401259
>>62395655
My IQ is >85

62402158
>>62395925
What's the tax rate there?

62402616
2x pump potential from here AT MOST, while the downside potential (probably $30k bottom) is terrible.

62402679
>>62402158
Same as USA cap gains but canada doesn't give you any incentive to hold long term. TFSA is sick though. I just treat my btc as zero so it keeps me aggressive on the risk curve with my TFSA.

62403029
>>62401192
Buy more. 100 btc

62403094
>>62400293
By native staking through protocols that prioritize self-custody. Simple.

62403095
>>62396002
>Invest only in actual products made by actual companies.
What are you investing?

62404024
>>62398163
Bank transfer? Hmmm

62404122
If you're not holding BTC when it is clear that the fiat system is on its last days you are beyond retarded.

62404297
>>62395655

Big Thai Cock?
Well I mean like why aren't YOU holding it?
I mean.

62404304
>why aren't you holding goy gold
/biz/ is already out of crypto.
https://web.archive.org/web/202602162103...f-bitcoin/

62404487
>>62404304
Lol

62404614
>>62404122
Why do you need fiat?

62405784
>>62401190
Why though? If you can earn from your BTC while maintaining self-custody, I don't see the problem.

62405818
>>62398819
If it's still BTC and I still control the keys, I don't see why it stops being Bitcoin.

62406014
>>62395655
>Why aren't you holding BTC?
I will buy when it's cheaper. 100 bucks seems like a fair value for the future of finance.

62406579
>>62399676
I guess not but..

62406783
There is simply no reason for it to have any value boss.

62409051
>>62399898
You are a fucking retard. The current price means NOTHING. Its NOTHING! Lower the better to buy more.
Boomers are still in the seats of power. They dont embrace BTC because most boomers not in some kind of power see it as not something they can walk into a bank and ask their financial advisor to get. Therefore its fake and gay to them.
The boomers being the biggest voting block, are the most catered to. So basically the reason why the world has not been embracing digital is because of these fucking idiots.
Once boomers are out of the seats of power and fucking gone and millennials are in charge its going to get real digital.BTC will inevitably be embraced.
More countries will just adopt it as their main currency like El Salvador. Its the best logical option. Your economy is crumbling and the nations dollar is shit? Just adopt bitcoin its quite simple.

Not only that, but owning a Bitcoin will be so rare, everything will cost in Satoshis. Once Amazon adopts a bitcoin logo payment option its 10000% on. Once Musk post some stupid twitter picture of a moon bitcoin mining farm its going to moon.

Get it now while its cheap. Price has no meaning at the moment. If your a day trader I get why you may be pessimistic about it. But if your a hodler and believe in BTC Philosophically and not thinking of it like a share/stock then thats the right idea.

BTC is known by everyone. If you went to what ever hood you are in and asked the most wet cigarette smelling schizo hobo " Hey have you heard of that digital gold thing" the response would be some schizo babble with "That bitcoin stuff:"

Its backed by million computers. It takes IRL power/electrical energy and time (mining) to produce. Thats what money realy is or better to say what gives something value. Time+Energy. Its basically electricity converted into money. And electricity is not free.

HODL is the right idea for a boomerless digital embraced future. dont let Day Traders do it for quick buck not future.

62409106
>>62395695
>"those companies work for your money."
>babby's first dable on stocks
>doesn't know owners are diluting your investment and buying mansions with the ATM

62409107
>>62399898
To add to this, You are thinking of like 10x+ full BTC holders. Average persons goal is to have a single one or 2 to just hold on to the future.
The scenario you are talking about is like for some kind of rich guy living on the lamb which is like 1 out of 10 million people.

The real persons goal is to get a bitcoin and maybe it will be like 300,000 per coin in the future. Then you have enough for your familys college or whatever. Just a good foundation for a son/daughters future. Not somthing you just cash out to cartoonishly and buy lambos with and post videos of your garage with hexagon light ceilings.

62409236
>>62405784
You know it's a risky move.

62409560
>>62409236
Depends what you mean by risky. I wouldn't touch an unaudited protocol, but I also don't think every new protocol carries the same level of risk.

62409564
>>62399898
Governments taxing something doesn't make it dead. If anything, it usually means they've accepted it's an asset class that's here to stay.

62409803
>>62409560
More like defi hacks

62409813
>>62409107
It's possible 10 years from now. Pass it to your kids

62409997
>>62409803
That's why I'm watching Babylon instead of blindly aping into random protocols. They've been taking audits and testing seriously.

62410003
>>62406014
People have gone broke waiting for the perfect entry just as often as they've bought too early.

item.title: ITT WE LAUGH AT SILVERNIGGERS AHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAH… — 55 replies — 70.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT WE LAUGH AT SILVERNIGGERS AHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAH…

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-06-25 07:54 UTC

OP

ITT WE LAUGH AT SILVERNIGGERS 

Early replies (first 3)

62406474
>>62406449
Not selling.

62406483
>>62406474

62406488
>>62406474
Quiet jamboy. The colonizers are laughing at your "investment".

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62407605
>>62406449
>>62407084
silverfags BTFO

62407646
>>62406527
same as bitcoin, spaceX and so on.
>good company i say!!!

62407704
Down more than bitcoin btw

62407728
>>62406526
>new silver is being mined at 20x the rate of gold
well that's just not true. I think the number is closer to 8x and even that might be a bit high.

and I think silver is being consumed at more than 8x the rate of gold, so it's going negative at current mining and consumption rates.

62407950
>>62406449
HOLY SHIT! MY SIDES! AAAAAHHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHAH LOL LMAO EVEN AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA BAGGIES ON SUICIDE WATCH! LOOOOL ROFL! MUH UTILITY! MUH FUTURE OF FUCKING FINANCE!

62407956
>>62407950
oops wrong thread

62407963
>>62406474

This. You say magic jewish number go down. But I still have the same number of ounces as yesterday.

62407970
>>62406474
that's called "bagholding"

62408007
I hold silver but I'm glad it's dropping, it's not an investment it's protection against the economy imploding, the cheaper it is the more I can buy

62408050
>>62408007
>I'm glad it's dropping
>so I can buy more
Lmao. Precious metals really are just Funko Pops for shutin schizophrenic virgin retards.

62408105
>>62406474
Based

>>62406449
Anyone whose held this for a good while will have patience and nerves like ice water. I never see BTC maxies talk about stacking Sats any more, perhaps because they know fundamentally its different. Zoom out, on the globe not the chart, the world is on fire and getting worse. Everything is getting hit, I posted here for weeks that the gold sell off was front running the stock market and bonds. Countries are going to be selling everything to stay alive, but Gold remains the most universally liquid without causing tge US to sperg. I'll say it again, where precious metals go you're going with it whether its crypto or NVDA, there is only a commodities bull left to invest in and even that will get hit. Cash chads stay winning but spend that money wisely at the everything sale; the US stock market had been too lucky and cancer has been allowed to grow. Normally recessions purge such things but its like when dead wood piles up due to a lack of forest fires. Also, food prices aren't going to be fully realized until october.

62408330
>>62406449
>>62406627
>>62406603
Final boss' desperation special. Something is coming. They wouldn't draw attention to themselves if it weren't serious.

62408343
>>62408050

what you dont understand here? its not a quick gamble...
so called "bagholding" was the plan all along!!

62408348
>>62408105
Just in time for me to start my bulk :')

62408419
>>62407950
kek cryptards are getting brain damage from being fucked so hard

62408483
>>62406449
i sold at the top

62408988
>>62406449
Didn't buy the top. Still in profit. Not selling.

62409027
>>62406449
I do love silver getting into slurping range

62409062
They bought silver because a rich indian proved its a scam,,,,,,lmao,,,,,,pol brain and investing is a deadly combination

62409141
>>62408105
lol N****r

62409263
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SO BULLISH AT SILVER
SUPPLY SHOCK WHEN????

62409270
>>62409263
Theres a 54 billion ounce glut of silver

62409295
>>62409263
>SUPPLY SHOCK WHEN
Comex and Shanghai exchange inventories have been going up now for a solid month. Industry is thrifting silver and replacing it as much as possible with copper and in silver just being a rock it has no economic value really compared to gold.

62409326
>>62406449
What retard didn't sell once that shit shot past $100?

62409335
>>62409326
The ones spamming the catalog about how smart they were

62409381
>>62409326
Stackturds all claim silver is going to $2000.....bunch of losers

62409428
>>62406449
Just count the minutes before the next global crisis and precious metals go back up

62409431
>>62409428
Like the iran war and silver plummeted....lmao nothing dumber or broker than a stacktard

62409935
>>62409428
>global crisis
>silver just dumps
LMAO

62409962
>>62409428
Silver and gold dump dring a crisis loljust look at how they dumped harder than the stock market during the financial crisis.

Looks like he lost a lot on another scamcoin, guess he better beg again.
Retarded ass mods.

item.title: Coinmetro finally bankrupt — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Coinmetro finally bankrupt

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-25 08:36 UTC

OP

HOW COULD YOU DO THIS TO US KEVIN?!
REEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE

Early replies (first 3)

62406510
OH NO NO NO ASSBLASTER BROS DID WE GOT PLAYED ALL THESE YEARS

62406517
Someone give me a tl;dr on this shitcoin. I remember browsing some threads way back and clearly seeing what a pile of shit it was, so I'm curious

62406551
>>62406517
it's too late anon

https://coinmetro.com/blog/announcement-onboarding-depositing-and-withdrawals

Due to an extraordinary situation caused by a failure of one of our financial service providers, Coinmetro OÜ has suspended new customer registrations, as well as deposit and withdrawal requests from existing customers from June 22nd, 2026.

We also disabled trading, to prevent any adverse consequences to the customers of Coinmetro, to Coinmetro itself and any other third parties.

To ensure fair and transparent treatment of all customers and an orderly handling of the situation, we have determined that the best available solution for our company will be to file a reorganisation application to the Estonian court.

Coinmetro OÜ is working on implementing a reorganisation plan in consultations with legal advisors. If the court decides to initiate a reorganisation process, this will help to manage remaining value of the existing technology and intellectual property of the business in the interests of the existing customers of Coinmetro OÜ.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409288
The AMA mentioned chapter 11. I knew CM was always full of shit.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=W6Ky-den7-k

62410028
There have been at least 3 threads on here about Coinmetro stopping transactions/folding, and none of them went past 10 replies so far
I'm surprised, if this was 4 or 5 years ago the thread would have easily hit the 300 reply limit, especially after all that shit-talking that Kevin had for Binance due to not being as regulation-compliant
Just goes to show how long the exchange was struggling and near-death. That XCM sell wall situation was embarrassing as hell. And now this happens.
Coinmetro is kill.
Forehead betrayed his believers.

item.title: >diversify into BTC, ETH, gold >they pulled me down so hard that it wiped out all my stock gains since jan '25 And I tho… — 12 replies — 13.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>diversify into BTC, ETH, gold >they pulled me down so hard that it wiped out all my stock gains since jan '25 And I tho…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-25 07:49 UTC

OP

>diversify into BTC, ETH, gold
>they pulled me down so hard that it wiped out all my stock gains since jan '25
And I thought I was smart for loading up at 70k after it fell from ATH

Early replies (first 3)

62407385
>>62407300
>diversify from high risk assets by buying giga high risk assets
???

62407437
>>62407300
you should sell all your crypto right now and then rebuy it all asap. the tax man cometh.

62408183
The cold hard truth is NOTHING is a good buy right now. MAYBE housing is, but literally nothing else. Crypto has zero hype ever returning to it (unlike the '22 crash which still had some hopeful bagholders hanging around), stocks are near bubble burst, precious metals turned into another pnd scheme, businesses have outrageously expensive overhead, etc.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408190
>>62407300
>fell for it again award

62408207
>>62408183
High interest accounts waiting to scoop up cheap deals after the bubble burst is the play

62408211
>>62408183
Cash is king. I only figured that out in May though :/

62409836
>>62407385
Gold isn't giga high risk. BTC and ETH will never hit 0 and both can be staked.
>>62408211
I have over 30k in cash with 4.5% yield, waiting to slurp. It feels like the bottom is nowhere near.

62409937
>>62409836
>facts proved him wrong
>explain him why he was wrong
>still doesn’t get it

62409943
>>62407300
>investment horizon of one year
when will you impulsive niggers ever learn

62409945
>>62408183
Stocks are fine, retard. There will probably be a pullback in the next 1-2 years, but it'll be like 10-15%, and it'll recover in 18 months. It will have precisely 0 effect on anyone investing long term.

You crypto farm animals deal with the most absurd volatility and risk of ruin the universe has ever seen. You deserve death simply because you are incapable of hearing the reasoning people have been shouting at you for years.

62409949
>>62409945
>tfw Anon has a crystal ball that actually works, and we don’t

62409954
>>62409949
Well, you can either bet on the one thing (stocks) that have survived many "catastrophes" and continued trucking along, because they are pinned to the productive output of 8 billion people. OR, you can invest in fake internet money that is pinned to nothing in the real world. Just the dreams of NEETs.

item.title: CSAM kills Bitcoin — 3 replies — 11.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
CSAM kills Bitcoin

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-25 08:25 UTC

OP

The first transaction that includes CSAM and is replicated across nodes will permanently tank Bitcoin's price.

The clock is ticking.

Early replies (first 3)

62410005
>>62410002
Adam looks glow, Gaylor is glowing too, is Lee a glow operation too?
Btvlw wtf is this in non alien vitalik language?

62410008
the missing use case already kills BTC lol. that is just desecrating the corpse

62410011
>>62410002
Not to mention once its committed it will be there forever, making the blockchain illegal to possess.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Smack My Lips Up — 7 replies — 11.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Smack My Lips Up

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-25 08:04 UTC

OP

$STRC has a 14.22% yield going by today's closing price.

George thinks we need to increase the dividend to drive volume. How high do we need the yield to be? Something sustainable like 25%?

Early replies (first 3)

62408877
max cope
its the same with junk bonds, the higher the yield the worse the bond!
>dump it right away, its cursed already!

62408897
>>62408877
No that's not how it works. With higher yield you get more money and more money isn't a bad thing. Maybe you should learn how finance works before posting on a finance board.

62408907
>>62408897
Are you not watching the required videos? We have to raise the dividend to get the yield up so people buy it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408926
>>62408835
Buy an add George.

62409154
>>62408907
The best investments are the ones you feel like a retard buying. It's called asymmetric returns.

62409269
>>62408926
We're all George.

62409974
>>62408835
>STEADY LADS, DEPLOYING MORE CAPITAL!
Vibes
